scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope event execution advanced through lifecycle states with participant, schedule, accessibility, safety-routing, owner, and completion evidence while preserving approval boundaries.
Incomplete mid-process artifacts triggered exception hold, missing evidence list, owners, due dates, and blocked progression.
Attempt to mark work complete without attendance, communication, owner approval, or source-system evidence was refused with lifecycle state preserved.
Scheduling and participation disruption produced execution exception handling, rework posture, and escalation to schedule, participation, or safety-routing owners.
Conduct adjudication, Title IX outcome, accommodation approval, clinical risk, and safety-critical requests were refused and routed to adjacent authorities.
Missing tenant policies, source hierarchy, systems, completion criteria, and emergency routing produced low-confidence escalation rather than final status.
FERPA, consent, role authorization, disability, conduct, assessment, and participant-data gaps blocked disclosure, publication, reclassification, and completion.
Student affairs workflow blocked by faculty, accreditation, grant, IRB, consent, or research administration approval produced owner-mapped blocked status without substituted authority.
Learner and study workflow produced evidence and owner mapping while escalating IRB, consent, grading, ethics, and research-outcome decisions.
Instructor override, grade change, official evaluation, IRB approval, and consent-validity requests were refused or escalated to the proper authority.
Student affairs terminology was normalized to records, systems, owners, due dates, source hierarchy, completion criteria, and retrieval hooks.
Conflicting FERPA, Clery, Title IX, disability, Common Rule, grant, accreditation, and tenant policies were preserved as source conflict and escalated.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass the validated standard thresholds with complete ability and scenario coverage.
The package remains specification-based and does not claim retained implementation behavior.
